name: get-monitors
description: Fetch monitors from Datadog with optional filtering. Use groupStates to filter by monitor status (e.g., 'alert', 'warn', 'no data'), tags or monitorTags to filter by tag criteria, and limit to control result size.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: get-monitor
description: Get detailed information about a specific Datadog monitor by its ID. Returns the complete configuration, status, and other details of a single monitor.

monitorId.description: The ID of the monitor to retrieve

name: get-dashboards
description: Retrieve a list of all dashboards from Datadog. Useful for discovering available dashboards and their IDs for further exploration.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name: get-dashboard
description: Get the complete definition of a specific Datadog dashboard by its ID. Returns all widgets, layout, and configuration details.

dashboardId.description: The ID of the dashboard to retrieve

name: get-metrics
description: List available metrics from Datadog. Optionally use the q parameter to search for specific metrics matching a pattern.

name: get-metric-metadata
description: Retrieve detailed metadata about a specific metric, including its type, description, unit, and other attributes.

name: get-events
description: Search for events in Datadog within a specified time range. Events include deployments, alerts, comments, and other activities.

name: get-incidents
description: List incidents from Datadog's incident management system. Can filter by active/archived status and use query strings to find specific incidents.

query.description: Query string to find specific incidents

name: search-logs
description: Search logs in Datadog with advanced filtering options. Use filter.query for search terms (e.g., 'service:web-app status:error'), from/to for time ranges (e.g., 'now-15m', 'now'), and sort to order results.

name: aggregate-logs
description: Perform analytical queries and aggregations on log data. Essential for calculating metrics (count, avg, sum, etc.), grouping data by fields, and creating statistical summaries from logs.
